# Rosalind Problem: Edit Distance Alignment in Scala

## Problem Understanding

The Edit Distance Alignment problem asks us to find the minimum number of operations (insertions, deletions, or substitutions) needed to transform one string into another, along with the actual alignment.

## Solution Approach

I'll use dynamic programming to solve this problem:
1. Build a matrix to compute edit distance
2. Trace back through the matrix to find the actual alignment
3. Return both the edit distance and the aligned strings

## Scala Implementation

```scala
object EditDistanceAlignment {
  
  def editDistanceAlignment(s1: String, s2: String): (Int, String, String) = {
    val m = s1.length
    val n = s2.length
    
    // Create DP matrix
    val dp = Array.ofDim[Int](m + 1, n + 1)
    
    // Initialize base cases
    for (i <- 0 to m) dp(i)(0) = i
    for (j <- 0 to n) dp(0)(j) = j
    
    // Fill the DP matrix
    for (i <- 1 to m) {
      for (j <- 1 to n) {
        val cost = if (s1(i - 1) == s2(j - 1)) 0 else 1
        dp(i)(j) = Seq(
          dp(i - 1)(j) + 1,        // deletion
          dp(i)(j - 1) + 1,        // insertion
          dp(i - 1)(j - 1) + cost   // substitution
        ).min
      }
    }
    
    // Trace back to find alignment
    var i = m
    var j = n
    var aligned1 = ""
    var aligned2 = ""
    
    while (i > 0 || j > 0) {
      if (i > 0 && j > 0 && s1(i - 1) == s2(j - 1)) {
        // Characters match, no operation needed
        aligned1 = s1(i - 1) + aligned1
        aligned2 = s2(j - 1) + aligned2
        i -= 1
        j -= 1
      } else if (i > 0 && j > 0 && dp(i)(j) == dp(i - 1)(j - 1) + 1) {
        // Substitution
        aligned1 = s1(i - 1) + aligned1
        aligned2 = s2(j - 1) + aligned2
        i -= 1
        j -= 1
      } else if (i > 0 && dp(i)(j) == dp(i - 1)(j) + 1) {
        // Deletion
        aligned1 = s1(i - 1) + aligned1
        aligned2 = "-" + aligned2
        i -= 1
      } else {
        // Insertion
        aligned1 = "-" + aligned1
        aligned2 = s2(j - 1) + aligned2
        j -= 1
      }
    }
    
    (dp(m)(n), aligned1, aligned2)
  }
  
  def main(args: Array[String]): Unit = {
    // Example usage
    val s1 = "PLEASANTLY"
    val s2 = "MEANLY"
    
    val (distance, alignment1, alignment2) = editDistanceAlignment(s1, s2)
    
    println(s"Edit Distance: $distance")
    println(s"Alignment 1: $alignment1")
    println(s"Alignment 2: $alignment2")
    
    // For Rosalind input format
    val input1 = "
